paper_summary.main_contribution: The paper demonstrates that selenium nanoparticles (SeNPs) biosynthesized using fenugreek seeds are effective in controlling Fusarium spp. infection in tomatoes, extending shelf life, and maintaining fruit quality without cytotoxic effects on normal cells.
paper_summary.narrative: The paper begins by characterizing SeNPs using XRD, FTIR, UV-Vis, TEM, and EDX (Fig. 1, Fig. 2), confirming their crystalline nature, spherical shape, and functional groups acting as stabilizers. The antifungal activity of SeNPs against Fusarium spp. is shown through significant inhibition zones and low MIC values (Table 1). Microscopic examination (Fig. 4) and treatment experiments (Fig. 3, Fig. 5) demonstrate SeNPs' ability to prevent fungal growth and maintain tomato quality. The safety of SeNPs is confirmed by their lack of cytotoxicity on normal cells (Fig. 6). The study further shows that SeNPs stabilize chemical properties and reduce weight loss in tomatoes (Fig. 8, Fig. 9), extending shelf life (Table 2). The preparation of tomatoes at different ripening stages (Fig. 7) supports the experimental setup.
paper_summary.limitations_noted: The study does not provide detailed statistical analysis for some figures, and the long-term effects of SeNPs on tomato quality and safety need further investigation.
